name: Sports
description: Teams, athletes, records, and sporting culture

questions.q: Which MLB team has won the most World Series championships?

source.notes: 27 titles as of 2024.

questions.q: Which player holds the MLB record for most home runs hit in a single season with 73?

source.notes: Set in 2001 with the San Francisco Giants.

questions.q: The Olympic motto 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' translates from Latin to what three words?

source.notes: 'Communiter' (Together) was added in 2021 by the IOC.

questions.q: The word 'marathon' comes from a legendary run in 490 BC associated with a battle in which country?

source.notes: Pheidippides ran from Marathon to Athens. The modern race is 26.2 miles / 42.195 km.

questions.q: Which city will host the 2028 Summer Olympic Games?

source.notes: LA's third time hosting (also 1932, 1984). Brisbane 2032.

questions.q: In which sport is a period of play called a 'chukka'?

source.notes: A match typically has 4-6 chukkas, each 7 minutes long.

questions.q: At the 1972 Munich Olympics, which swimmer won seven gold medals in a single Games?

source.notes: Phelps later broke this with 8 golds at 2008 Beijing.

questions.q: The 'Fosbury Flop,' a technique of going over the bar backwards, revolutionized which track and field event?

source.notes: Dick Fosbury won gold at 1968 Mexico City Olympics with this technique.

questions.q: In a relay race, what object is passed between runners?

source.notes: Must be exchanged within a 20-meter changeover zone.

questions.q: Camp Nou, one of the largest football stadiums in Europe, is the home ground of which club?

source.notes: Capacity ~99,354. Currently being renovated as 'Spotify Camp Nou.'

questions.q: Which NBA player holds the record for the most career MVP awards with six?
Which NBA franchise has won the most championships in league history?

source.notes: 18 titles through 2024, one more than the Lakers' 17.

questions.q: Who became the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in February 2023?

source.notes: Passed Kareem's 38,387 points on Feb 7, 2023.

questions.q: Which player scored 100 points in a single NBA game in 1962, a record that still stands?

source.notes: March 2, 1962. Philadelphia Warriors vs. New York Knicks. Score: 169-147.

questions.q: At 5 feet 3 inches, who is the shortest player in NBA history?

source.notes: Played 14 NBA seasons (1987-2001), mostly with the Charlotte Hornets.

questions.q: Which NBA team holds the record for the longest winning streak at 33 consecutive games?

source.notes: 1971-72 season. Warriors hold the best season record (73-9 in 2015-16).

questions.q: The three weapons used in competitive fencing are the foil, the epee, and which third weapon?

source.notes: Foil: torso target. Epee: whole body. Sabre: above waist including head.

questions.q: Churchill Downs, home of the Kentucky Derby, is located in which city?

source.notes: First Kentucky Derby held in 1875.

questions.q: Professional golfer Rory McIlroy represents which part of the United Kingdom?

source.notes: Born in Holywood, County Down. 4-time major champion.

questions.q: The Dutch national football team is known for playing in which distinctive jersey color?

source.notes: From the House of Orange-Nassau, the Dutch royal family.
